name: Vertical Formatting
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 5: Formatting
problem: Source files lack consistent vertical spacing, causing conceptually distinct sections to blur together and related code to be separated by unrelated lines, making the visual structure of the code misleading.
steps: Separate each distinct concept or function with a blank line to create visual breathing room.
Keep lines of code that are closely related (e.g., variable declaration and first use) vertically close without blank lines between them.
Declare local variables immediately before their first use, not at the top of a function.
Declare instance variables at the top of the class, grouped together.
Never let a source file grow beyond ~500 lines; most should be under 200.
